Ohio here… at what level storm do you typically have to evacuate?

depends on where you are in FL. Run from water. Hide from the wind. I’m not in a flood zone and 20 miles inland or so. I’m in a house built after 2015. I likely would never evacuate. It would never be ordered here anyway.

Usually you’re ordered to evacuate based on your flood zone. There is a map that tells you. Your county puts out a notice saying which zones need to evacuate for floods. Any level storm can bring floods. For wind, typically for a category 1 or above, mobile/manufactured homes are ordered to evacuate. Between category 1 and 3, there is a lot of discretion for those who live in regular housing. Some people evacuate for everything, some prefer to shelter in place but will evacuate for a cat 3 or higher, it kind of depends on your situation and the individual storm. You have a few days to watch what it’s doing. There is a bit of a gamble as the storm’s trajectory can change; I remember one storm (I think Charlie) where a lot of people evacuated into its path. Gas is a commodity, as is space on the road, so everybody can’t evacuate every time. I’ve never experienced being in the path of a category 4 or 5, but I think mandatory evacuations may apply for those almost always.
